001package guiAddRemoveRoles;
002
003import java.util.ArrayList;
004import java.util.List;
005import javafx.beans.value.ObservableValue;
006import javafx.collections.FXCollections;
007import javafx.geometry.Pos;
008import javafx.scene.Scene;
009import javafx.scene.control.Alert;
010import javafx.scene.control.Button;
011import javafx.scene.control.ComboBox;
012import javafx.scene.control.Label;
013import javafx.scene.layout.Pane;
014import javafx.scene.shape.Line;
015import javafx.scene.text.Font;
016import javafx.stage.Stage;
017import database.Database;
018import entityClasses.User;
019
020/*******
021 * <p> Title: GUIAddRemoveRolesPage Class. </p>
022 * 
023 * <p> Description: The Java/FX-based page for changing the assigned roles to users.</p>
024 * 
025 * <p> Copyright: Lynn Robert Carter © 2025 </p>
026 * 
027 * @author Lynn Robert Carter
028 * 
029 * @version 1.00                2025-08-20 Initial version
030 *  
031 */
032
033public class ViewAddRemoveRoles {
034        
035        /*-*******************************************************************************************
036
037        Attributes
038        
039        */
040        
041        // These are the application values required by the user interface
042        
043        private static double width = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_WIDTH;
044        private static double height = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_HEIGHT;
045
046        
047        // These are the widget attributes for the GUI. There are 3 areas for this GUI.
048        
049        // GUI Area 1: It informs the user about the purpose of this page, whose account is being used,
050        // and a button to allow this user to update the account settings.
051        protected static Label label_PageTitle = new Label();
052        protected static Label label_UserDetails = new Label();
053        protected static Button button_UpdateThisUser = new Button("Account Update");
054        
055        // This is a separator and it is used to partition the GUI for various tasks
056        protected static Line line_Separator1 = new Line(20, 95, width-20, 95);
057        
058        // When no user has been selected, only Area 2a is shown.  If a user in the ComboBox in Area 1a
059        // has been specified, then Area 2b is made visible.
060        
061        // Area 2a: This allows the admin to select a user of the system as the first step in adding or
062        // removing a role.  The act of selecting a user causes the change is the GUI.  The Admin does
063        // not need to push a button to make this happen.
064        protected static Label label_SelectUser = new Label("Select a user to be updated:");
065        protected static ComboBox <String> combobox_SelectUser = new ComboBox <String>();
066        
067        // Area 2b: When a user has been selected these widgets are shown and can be used
068        protected static List<String> addList = new ArrayList<String>();
069        protected static Button button_AddRole = new Button("Add This Role");
070        protected static List<String> removeList = new ArrayList<String>();
071        protected static Button button_RemoveRole = new Button("Remove This Role");
072        protected static Label label_CurrentRoles = new Label("This user's current roles:");
073        protected static Alert alertRoleError = new Alert(Alert.AlertType.INFORMATION);
074        protected static Label label_SelectRoleToBeAdded = new Label("Select a role to be added:");
075        protected static ComboBox <String> combobox_SelectRoleToAdd = new ComboBox <String>();  
076        protected static Label label_SelectRoleToBeRemoved = new Label("Select a role to be removed:");
077        protected static ComboBox <String> combobox_SelectRoleToRemove = new ComboBox <String>();
078                
079        // This is a separator and it is used to partition the GUI for various tasks
080        protected static Line line_Separator4 = new Line(20, 525, width-20,525);
081        
082        // GUI Area 3: This is last of the GUI areas.  It is used for quitting the application, logging
083        // out, and on other pages a return is provided so the user can return to a previous page when
084        // the actions on that page are complete.  Be advised that in most cases in this code, the 
085        // return is to a fixed page as opposed to the actual page that invoked the pages.
086        protected static Button button_Return = new Button("Return");
087        protected static Button button_Logout = new Button("Logout");
088        protected static Button button_Quit = new Button("Quit");
089
090        // This is the end of the GUI objects for the page.
091        
092        // These attributes are used to configure the page and populate it with this user's information
093        private static ViewAddRemoveRoles theView;      // Used to determine if instantiation of the class
094                                                                                                // is needed
095        // Reference for the in-memory database so this package has access
096        private static Database theDatabase = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.database;         
097
098        protected static Stage theStage;                        // The Stage that JavaFX has established for us
099        protected static Pane theRootPane;                      // The Pane that holds all the GUI widgets 
100        protected static User theUser;                          // The current user of the application
101        
102        public static Scene theAddRemoveRolesScene = null;      // The Scene each invocation populates
103        protected static String theSelectedUser = "";   // The user whose roles are being updated
104        protected static String theAddRole = "";                // The role being added
105        protected static String theRemoveRole = "";             // The roles being removed
106
107
108
109        /*-*******************************************************************************************
110
111        Constructors
112        
113        */
114
115        /**********
116         * <p> Method: displayAddRemoveRoles(Stage ps, User user) </p>
117         * 
118         * <p> Description: This method is the single entry point from outside this package to cause
119         * the AddRevove page to be displayed.
120         * 
121         * It first sets up very shared attributes so we don't have to pass parameters.
122         * 
123         * It then checks to see if the page has been setup.  If not, it instantiates the class, 
124         * initializes all the static aspects of the GUI widgets (e.g., location on the page, font,
125         * size, and any methods to be performed).
126         * 
127         * After the instantiation, the code then populates the elements that change based on the user
128         * and the system's current state.  It then sets the Scene onto the stage, and makes it visible
129         * to the user.
130         * 
131         * @param ps specifies the JavaFX Stage to be used for this GUI and it's methods
132         * 
133         * @param user specifies the User whose roles will be updated
134         *
135         */
136        public static void displayAddRemoveRoles(Stage ps, User user) {
137                
138                // Establish the references to the GUI and the current user
139                theStage = ps;
140                theUser = user;
141                
142                // If not yet established, populate the static aspects of the GUI by creating the 
143                // singleton instance of this class
144                if (theView == null) theView = new ViewAddRemoveRoles();
145                
146                // Default to no user selected
147                combobox_SelectUser.getSelectionModel().select(0);
148                
149                // Populate the dynamic aspects of the GUI with the data from the user and the current
150                // state of the system.  This page is different from the others.  Since there are two 
151                // modes (1: user has not been selected, and 2: user has been selected) there are two
152                // lists of widgets to be displayed.  For this reason, we have implemented the following 
153                // two controller methods to deal with this dynamic aspect.
154                ControllerAddRemoveRoles.repaintTheWindow();
155                ControllerAddRemoveRoles.doSelectUser();
156        }
157
158        
159        /**********
160         * <p> Method: GUIAddRemoveRolesPage() </p>
161         * 
162         * <p> Description: This method initializes all the elements of the graphical user interface.
163         * This method determines the location, size, font, color, and change and event handlers for
164         * each GUI object. </p>
165         * 
166         * This is a singleton, so this is performed just one.  Subsequent uses fill in the changeable
167         * fields using the displayAddRempoveRoles method.</p>
168         * 
169         */
170        public ViewAddRemoveRoles() {
171                
172                // This page is used by all roles, so we do not specify the role being used             
173                        
174                // Create the Pane for the list of widgets and the Scene for the window
175                theRootPane = new Pane();
176                theAddRemoveRolesScene = new Scene(theRootPane, width, height);
177                
178                // Populate the window with the title and other common widgets and set their static state
179                
180                // GUI Area 1
181                label_PageTitle.setText("Add/Removed Roles Page");
182                setupLabelUI(label_PageTitle, "Arial", 28, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 5);
183
184                label_UserDetails.setText("User: " + theUser.getUserName());
185                setupLabelUI(label_UserDetails, "Arial", 20, width, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 20, 55);
186                
187                setupButtonUI(button_UpdateThisUser, "Dialog", 18, 170, Pos.CENTER, 610, 45);
188                button_UpdateThisUser.setOnAction((_) -> 
189                        {guiUserUpdate.ViewUserUpdate.displayUserUpdate(theStage, theUser); });
190                
191                alertRoleError.setTitle("Role Update Error");
192                alertRoleError.setHeaderText(null);
193                
194                // GUI Area 2a
195                setupLabelUI(label_SelectUser, "Arial", 20, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 20, 130);
196                
197                setupComboBoxUI(combobox_SelectUser, "Dialog", 16, 250, 280, 125);
198                List<String> userList = theDatabase.getUserList();      
199                combobox_SelectUser.setItems(FXCollections.observableArrayList(userList));
200                combobox_SelectUser.getSelectionModel().select(0);
201                combobox_SelectUser.getSelectionModel().selectedItemProperty()
202        .addListener((@SuppressWarnings("unused") ObservableValue<? extends String> observable, 
203                @SuppressWarnings("unused") String oldvalue, 
204                @SuppressWarnings("unused") String newValue) -> {ControllerAddRemoveRoles.doSelectUser();});
205                
206                // GUI Area 2b
207                setupLabelUI(label_CurrentRoles, "Arial", 16, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 50, 170); 
208                setupLabelUI(label_SelectRoleToBeAdded, "Arial", 20, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 20, 210);
209                setupComboBoxUI(combobox_SelectRoleToAdd, "Dialog", 16, 150, 280, 205);
210                setupButtonUI(button_AddRole, "Dialog", 16, 150, Pos.CENTER, 460, 205);
211                ViewAddRemoveRoles.button_AddRole.setOnAction((_) -> 
212                        {ControllerAddRemoveRoles.performAddRole(); });
213                setupButtonUI(button_RemoveRole, "Dialog", 16, 150, Pos.CENTER, 460, 275);                      
214                ViewAddRemoveRoles.button_RemoveRole.setOnAction((_) -> 
215                        {ControllerAddRemoveRoles.performRemoveRole(); });
216                setupLabelUI(label_SelectRoleToBeRemoved, "Arial", 20, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 20, 280);        
217                setupComboBoxUI(combobox_SelectRoleToRemove, "Dialog", 16, 150, 280, 275);      
218                
219                // GUI Area 3           
220                setupButtonUI(button_Return, "Dialog", 18, 210, Pos.CENTER, 20, 540);
221                button_Return.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerAddRemoveRoles.performReturn(); });
222
223                setupButtonUI(button_Logout, "Dialog", 18, 210, Pos.CENTER, 300, 540);
224                button_Logout.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerAddRemoveRoles.performLogout(); });
225    
226                setupButtonUI(button_Quit, "Dialog", 18, 210, Pos.CENTER, 570, 540);
227                button_Quit.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerAddRemoveRoles.performQuit(); });
228                
229                // This is the end of the GUI Widgets for the page
230                
231                // Due to the very dynamic nature of this page, setting the widget into the Root Pane has 
232                // has been delegated to the repaintTheWindow and doSelectUser controller methods.
233                // Don't follow this pattern if formatting of the page does not change dynamically.
234        }       
235
236        /*-*******************************************************************************************
237
238        Helper methods used to minimizes the number of lines of code needed above
239        
240        */
241
242        /**********
243         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a label
244         * 
245         * @param l             The Label object to be initialized
246         * @param ff    The font to be used
247         * @param f             The size of the font to be used
248         * @param w             The width of the Button
249         * @param p             The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
250         * @param x             The location from the left edge (x axis)
251         * @param y             The location from the top (y axis)
252         */
253        
254        private static void setupLabelUI(Label l, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x,
255                        double y){
256                l.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
257                l.setMinWidth(w);
258                l.setAlignment(p);
259                l.setLayoutX(x);
260                l.setLayoutY(y);                
261        }
262        
263        
264        /**********
265         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a button
266         * 
267         * @param b             The Button object to be initialized
268         * @param ff    The font to be used
269         * @param f             The size of the font to be used
270         * @param w             The width of the Button
271         * @param p             The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
272         * @param x             The location from the left edge (x axis)
273         * @param y             The location from the top (y axis)
274         */
275        protected static void setupButtonUI(Button b, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x,
276                        double y){
277                b.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
278                b.setMinWidth(w);
279                b.setAlignment(p);
280                b.setLayoutX(x);
281                b.setLayoutY(y);                
282        }
283
284        /**********
285         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a ComboBox
286         * 
287         * @param c             The ComboBox object to be initialized
288         * @param ff    The font to be used
289         * @param f             The size of the font to be used
290         * @param w             The width of the ComboBox
291         * @param x             The location from the left edge (x axis)
292         * @param y             The location from the top (y axis)
293         */
294        protected static void setupComboBoxUI(ComboBox <String> c, String ff, double f, double w,
295                        double x, double y){
296                c.setStyle("-fx-font: " + f + " " + ff + ";");
297                c.setMinWidth(w);
298                c.setLayoutX(x);
299                c.setLayoutY(y);
300        }
301}
